channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden rule requiring serial numbers and background checks for ghost guns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday upheld a Biden administration regulation on the nearly impossible-to-trace weapons called ghost guns, clearing the way for continued serial numbers, background checks and age verification requirements for buying the kits online.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump will announce auto tariffs at a White House news conference
item.description: President Donald Trump will announce tariffs on auto imports, a move the White Houses claims would foster domestic manufacturing that could also put a financial squeeze on automakers that depend on global supply chains.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hakeem Jeffries calls for Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to resign
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries tells NewsNation that Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth should lose his job.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard calls Signal chats a 'mistake' as Trump officials face grilling over leaked military plan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard said Wednesday it was a “mistake” for national security officials to discuss sensitive military plans on a group text chain that also included a journalist — a leak that has roiled President Donald Trump's national security leadership
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court for OK to cut teacher-training money as part of anti-DEI push
item.description: The Trump administration is at the Supreme Court with a new emergency appeal, this time seeking approval to go ahead with cuts of hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court for OK to cut teacher-training money as part of anti-DEI push
item.description: The Trump administration has asked the Supreme Court to allow it to cut hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hunter Biden hires Alex Murdaugh’s lawyer in latest court case; Abbe Lowell out
item.description: South Carolina attorney Richard A. Harpootlian is Hunter Biden's new counsel, as Abbe Lowell has reportedly been dropped from a defamation case being heard in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOGE says it needs to know the government’s most sensitive data, but can’t say why
item.description: DOGE staffers have skirted privacy laws, training and security protocols to gain virtually unfettered access to financial and personal information stored in siloed government databases.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler and Jenna McLaughlin, MPR News

item.title: The Latest: Republican Senate Armed Services chairman wants investigation into Signal chat
item.description: Senate Armed Services Chairman Roger Wicker and the committee's ranking Democrat, Sen. Jack Reed, are requesting an inspector general investigation into the use of Signal by top national security officials to discuss military plans.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In their own words: Trump officials shrugging off Signal leak once decried Clinton's server
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The attempts by President Donald Trump and top leaders of his administration to downplay a security breach that revealed military strike plans in a Signal group chat including a journalist stand in stark contrast to their reaction to Hillary Clinton's use of a home server as secret
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How the Signal 'group chat' furor has affected the 5 biggest players
item.description: The fact that such inherently sensitive information was being communicated in such a forum — and in front of a reporter whom no other participant appears to have noticed — has caused a political storm.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Homeland Security Secretary visits El Salvador prison where deported Venezuelans are held
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem is visiting the high-security El Salvador prison where Venezuelans who the Trump administration alleges are members of the Venezuelan Tren de Aragua gang have been held since their removal from the U_S_ Noem’s Wednesday trip to the prison where inmates are pac
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo talks possible Medicaid cuts, education, movie studios
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo said he may have to call a special session later in the year to handle possible cuts from the federal government that directly impact the state budget.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oz moves closer to confirmation as Medicare, Medicaid chief
item.description: Mehmet Oz is a step closer to being confirmed as the next head of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, after the Senate Finance Committee on Tuesday advanced his nomination. The vote was 14-13 along party lines.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: US infrastructure improved with Biden-era spending but there's a long way to go
item.description: A report card on the upkeep of America's infrastructure gave it a “C” grade on Tuesday, up slightly from previous years.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Another poll puts NYC mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani in 2nd place
item.description: Editor's note: The video above originally aired on Feb. 28. NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- With the New York City mayor's race heating up, Assemblymember Zohran Mamdani is polling ahead of Mayor Eric Adams, according to a poll by Honan Strategy Group.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Democrats slam Pete Hegseth over military strike plan texted to journalist
item.description: “There is no world in which this information should have been shared in non-secure channels,” said Rep. Seth Moulton (D-Mass.).
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump officials texted war plans to a group chat in a secure app that included a journalist
item.description: Top national security officials for President Donald Trump, including his defense secretary, texted war plans for upcoming military strikes in Yemen to a group chat in a secure messaging app that included the editor-in-chief for The Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Atlantic journalist says Trump officials included him in war plans group chat
item.description: Jeffrey Goldberg, editor in chief of The Atlantic, was apparently included in a Trump administration group chat on Signal in which top officials debated and then discussed details of attacks against Houthi rebels in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Letter carriers speak out against Trump plans to privatize U.S. Postal Service
item.description: Demonstrators rallied in St. Paul and Duluth on Sunday against calls to privatize the U.S. Postal Service. U.S. Sen. Tina Smith called the move a “hostile takeover” by Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Ki and Alex Haddon, MPR News

item.title: The Pentagon's DEI purge: Officials describe a scramble to remove and then restore online content
item.description: The Pentagon has faced questions from lawmakers, local leaders and citizens over the removal of military heroes and historic mentions from Defense Department websites and social media pages.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: DOJ creating path for people with criminal convictions to own guns again
item.description: The Justice Department plans to create a process for those with criminal convictions to restore their gun rights, sparking alarm it will return firearms to those convicted of violent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
